addition_policy.atomic_or_equivalence: Each appended claim must independently support the complete atomic statement; two partial claims cannot be combined inside one OR option.
addition_policy.negative_or_equivalence: Each appended claim must independently anchor the declared exclusion, contrast, or failure boundary.
addition_policy.operation: Append the listed claim IDs to the named option groups while preserving every parent option and statement unchanged.
addition_policy.paper_identity: Every atomic alternative remains in the same required paper as the parent anchor so required-paper and required-source identities remain unchanged.
addition_policy.partial_conjunctions: A claim that supports only one conjunct is rejected even when another reviewed claim could supply the missing conjunct.

append_claims.or_equivalence_rationale: It directly states that GraphRAG corrects some standalone-model errors but also turns some originally correct standalone answers wrong, establishing bidirectional complementarity and no universal winner.
It explicitly describes the evidence as conditional guidance rather than proof that GraphRAG is universally superior.
It independently specifies applying the indexing pipeline to a new document and unioning the new nodes and edges with the existing graph.
It independently states that new extracted nodes and edges are added without reconstructing the existing index graph.
It directly contrasts adding knowledge as graph associations with rerunning hierarchical summarization over the corpus.
It independently says real-time updates are future work and that incremental-update latency and consistency were not benchmarked.
It explicitly reports degradation as graph edges are removed, so it independently refutes zero degradation and universal robustness.
It independently establishes that wrong or incomplete triples propagate into answer errors, demonstrating a corruption mode not covered by the edge-removal result.
It independently states both sides of the candidate-count trade-off: too few omit evidence and too many introduce distraction.
It directly reports non-monotonic sensitivity to sentence inclusion, including the noise-before-benefit pattern.
It independently reports that 32k outperformed 64k and attributes the loss to diluted subtle extraction signals.
It shows that both larger and smaller chunks can lose information, anchoring the warning that a reported chunk optimum is not monotonic or universal.
It supplies a direct within-system counterexample in which a larger context window performs worse, anchoring the non-generalization warning.
It independently distinguishes structural pooling in the graph token from node-and-edge detail preserved by textualization, establishing complementary evidence roles.
It independently reports substantial ablation losses when either the graph encoder or textualized graph is removed, directly supporting complementarity.
It directly reports that path-organized prompts outperform flat organization and attributes the result to preserved relational order.
It independently limits thematic summaries to global coverage rather than precise local citation recovery, supporting the need to retain citation-level detail.
Its distinct structural-token and explicit-detail roles independently refute the claim that structure alone preserves every answer-relevant detail.
Its two removal ablations independently refute treating either representation as sufficient by itself.
Its path-versus-flat result independently refutes treating flattened facts as lossless for relational reasoning.
Its global-summary versus precise-citation boundary independently refutes treating high-level summaries as sufficient for every answer.
It independently states that stage-specific metrics distinguish graph-construction, retrieval, and generation failures.
It independently defines open mode as permitting parametric knowledge and reject mode as requiring abstention when retrieval is insufficient.
It independently states that corpus-level questions lack straightforward ground truth and therefore the study relies on an LLM judge.
The open-versus-reject distinction independently anchors the warning that permissive answer accuracy is not proof of successful retrieval.
Its explicit no-straightforward-ground-truth and LLM-judge dependence independently anchor the warning not to treat the judge as unquestionable truth.
It independently states that dependency parsing misses implicit or context-dependent relations absent from surface syntax.
It independently states that sharing a schema aligns graph extraction with query decomposition.
It directly anchors the negative boundary that surface-syntax dependency parsing cannot be assumed to capture implicit relations.
Its extraction-to-query schema alignment independently anchors the warning against optimizing an index whose vocabulary the query decomposer cannot address.
It independently specifies logic-form decomposition first and dual-level fuzzy fallback when decomposition or verification fails.
Its explicit structured-first and fuzzy-fallback contract independently anchors the warning not to collapse failure fallback into undifferentiated graph search.
It independently reports the Legal example's fewer-than-100 keyword tokens and one call, together with the exact GraphRAG 610-report comparison.
It independently binds GraphRAG's token percentages to its graph-free source-text summarization baseline, anchoring the baseline-specific caution.
It independently binds LightRAG's cost figures to a specific GraphRAG Legal example, anchoring the warning against cross-study league tables.
It independently specifies filtering GraphRAG and standalone-model answer sets by logits and combining the retained candidates.
It independently reports that pre-generation argument checking is preferred over post-generation result checking.
It independently defines the open policy and the retrieval-grounded reject policy with mandatory abstention on insufficient evidence.
Its reject-mode abstention requirement directly anchors the prohibition on silently substituting parametric knowledge when evidence is insufficient.
Its confidence-filtered answer-set fusion independently anchors the distinction between candidate confidence fusion and support verification.
Its direct pre-generation versus post-generation comparison independently anchors the distinction between evidence support and completed-answer checking.

rejected_close_alternatives.reason: It does not independently state both the basic-RAG comparability result and graph-expansion noise.
It classifies task levels but does not independently report GraphRAG's advantage.
It concerns a different system and does not establish CommunityKG-RAG's rapid-update claim and missing protocol.
It concerns a different system and not the dependency framework's unspecified lifecycle.
It concerns a different system and does not establish deletion, provenance-repair, or re-indexing behavior.
It is a different system's limitation and is not OR-equivalent to the named dependency framework claim.
It omits the exact 50 percent condition and the named LightRAG and NaiveRAG baselines, although it is sufficient for q033-n1.
It omits the exact 30-path condition and the quantitative recovery details.
It establishes error propagation but omits the faster graph-only route conjunct, although it is sufficient for q033-n1.
It establishes the speed trade-off but omits wrong or incomplete SPO facts as the cause.
It broadly coordinates resources but does not independently state that original chunks recover missing detail and generation context.
It states the large-k distraction side but not the too-few-candidates omission side as completely as the approved claim.
It supports only the excessive-initial-node conjunct.
It supports only the chunk-size conjunct and cannot satisfy the composite atomic group by itself.
It states a best configuration but not the non-monotonic sentence-inclusion result.
It establishes two representations but not their complementary contribution.
It explains information loss from flattening but does not independently report the path-versus-flat outperforming result.
It is a broad resource-organization synthesis and does not independently establish missing-detail recovery through original chunks.
It states stage diagnosis but omits the required metric families.
It defines open and reject modes but does not independently state that permissive prompting can mask retrieval failure; it is approved for q036-a3 and q036-n1.
It is semantically close but belongs to another paper, which would break the frozen required-paper and required-source identity contract.
It is semantically close but belongs to another paper, which would break the frozen required-paper and required-source identity contract.
It concerns general LLM-judge uncertainty in another paper and does not preserve the atomic group's required source identity.
It reports cost-performance behavior but not interchangeable dependency and LLM construction modes.
It motivates dependency extraction for cost and refresh but not the complete interchangeable-mode contract.
It supports only one side of the open-extraction alignment versus schema-expert-cost contrast.
It supports only one side of the open-extraction alignment versus schema-expert-cost contrast.
It covers only the entity-key routing branch.
It covers only the relation-or-theme routing branch.
It covers schema-valid decomposition but not all execution routes; pairing it with another claim is not valid OR semantics.
It covers the execution routes but not schema-valid decomposition; pairing it with another claim is not valid OR semantics.
It supplies the token percentages but omits the reported vector-RAG quality advantage; it is approved for q039-n1.
It omits the exact 10-to-30-times cheaper and 6-to-13-times faster ratios.
It reports token use but omits the substantially stronger task-score trade-off.
It establishes separate answer sources but not confidence thresholds and retained-candidate fusion.
It discusses candidate answers but not the complete threshold-and-fusion policy.
It describes the checking procedure but not the reported pre-generation versus post-generation comparison.
It establishes prompt leakage but not the reject-mode retrieval-grounding and abstention contract.
